summary.intent: Fix combobox edge cases identified by a second Codex review pass on PR #669 in fogodev/ars-ui, covering custom value preservation, blur ordering, pointer clicks, and item clicks after blur.
summary.outcome: All four Codex-identified edge cases were addressed with regression tests and implementation fixes; cargo xci --profile fast passed all 10 gates locally, and the fix was committed as a new commit on the branch with the changes pushed to PR #669.

repo: The combobox component lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs and has a corresponding spec at spec/components/selection/combobox.md that must be kept in sync.
The project uses a custom `cargo xci --profile fast` command as a local CI gate with 10 checks including fmt, clippy, adapter tests, and snapshot-count.
cargo xtask spec validate is used to validate spec files against implementation.
The PR workflow involves a `snapshot-reviewed` label and a `@codex review` trigger comment pattern to initiate Codex automated review cycles.
Codex review threads must be explicitly replied to and resolved via GitHub API after each fix cycle before triggering a new review pass.
GitHub's PR checks summary lags behind actual job step completion; direct job step polling via gh api is needed for accurate status.

code.finding: invalidate_collection_references and retain_present_selection are key state machine functions for handling item updates and custom value retention during blur/update cycles.
commit_input_plan / apply_custom_input_commit handles the custom value commit path; the signature for retain_present_selection needed updating as part of the fix.
on_input_focus, on_input_blur, on_input_change, and on_item_click are the primary event handlers; an on_input_click helper was missing and needed to be added.
Blur and custom value behavior sections in the spec required updating to match the new implementation, particularly around UpdateItems/retain/stale/custom value semantics.

workflow: Red-green-refactor discipline was followed: regression tests were written first, confirmed failing (compile errors counted), then implementation was fixed.
Multiple apply_patch rounds are often needed for a single logical fix due to dependent changes across the state machine.
The Codex review cycle is iterative: each push triggers a new automated review that may surface additional issues, requiring another fix-commit-push loop.
Monitoring both gh pr checks and direct GitHub Actions job step APIs in parallel gives faster and more accurate CI status than relying on the PR checks summary alone.

friction: GitHub's PR checks summary consistently lagged behind actual job completion, requiring direct Actions API polling to get accurate status.
The CI pipeline is slow enough that multiple 90-180 second sleep/poll cycles were needed between checks.
Codex opened 4 new review threads after the first fix cycle, requiring a full second round of regression tests, implementation changes, and CI waits.
The missing on_input_click helper caused compile errors before behavioral test failures could even be observed, slowing the red-green cycle.
Multiple apply_patch iterations were needed due to interdependent state machine changes across the combobox implementation.
open_items: Coverage, Feature Flags Dioxus, and Release Verification CI checks were still pending when the second fix was committed — their final status was not confirmed in the transcript.
Codex had not yet left a final approval reaction by the end of the session; a new review cycle for the second fix commit will be needed.
The clippy warning introduced during the fix cycle was noted as fixed, but the full clippy gate on the pushed commit has not yet been confirmed green in CI.
